q:

A. In the car.
B. Behind the crash barriers.
C. On the hard shoulder.

The safest place is behind the crash barriers.

What do you see on this sign?

B. The number of a motorway.
C. The number of a regular way.

It is an international road.
In Belgium that are all motorways, but abroad, that are sometimes ordinary roads.

q:
Are you allowed to stop on a motorway at this sign to send an urgent message?

A. Yes.
B. No.
e:
LESSON 4
On a motorway, you may only stop on an emergency bay if your vehicle breaks down or in case of a real emergency. Sending a message, even an urgent one, is not a valid reason. Stopping without a justified reason is dangerous and prohibited.

Are you allowed to stop on a motorway at this sign to send an urgent message?

B. No.

LESSON 4
On a motorway, you may only stop on an emergency bay if your vehicle breaks down or in case of a real emergency. Sending a message, even an urgent one, is not a valid reason. Stopping without a justified reason is dangerous and prohibited.

q:
What does this road sign indicate?

A. An exit.
B. A traffic interchange.
e:
LESSON 4 - 'Motorway'.
A traffic interchange, because you can drive from one motorway to another without any problem.
clean_q:
What does this road sign indicate?
A. An exit.

clean_e:
LESSON 4 - 'Motorway'.
A traffic interchange, because you can drive from one motorway to another without any problem.

q:
Must you use your indicator when you enter the motorway via the acceleration lane?

A. Yes.
B. No, because you can only drive in that direction.
e:
LESSON 4
What does Article 13 of the traffic regulations say?
Before carrying out a manoeuvre or a movement that requires a lateral displacement or a change of direction, the driver must indicate their intention in good time by using the direction indicators, if the vehicle is equipped with them, or otherwise, if possible, by a hand signal. This indication must stop as soon as the lateral movement or change of direction has been completed.
clean_q:
Must you use your indicator when you enter the motorway via the acceleration lane?
A. Yes.
B. No, because you can only drive in that direction.
clean_e:
LESSON 4
What does Article 13 of the traffic regulations say?
Before carrying out a manoeuvre or a movement that requires a lateral displacement or a change of direction, the driver must indicate their intention in good time by using the direction indicators, if the vehicle is equipped with them, or otherwise, if possible, by a hand signal. This indication must stop as soon as the lateral movement or change of direction has been completed.

q:
The photo is taken from the car you are driving.
You want to enter the motorway.
Which statement is correct?

A. You must drive to the very end of the acceleration lane.
B. You must give way.
C. You have priority when merging.
e:
LESSON 4
When you enter a motorway via an acceleration lane,
you must give way to traffic already driving on the motorway.
That is why there is a yield (inverted triangle) road sign.
You are not required to drive to the end of the acceleration lane.
You may merge as soon as you have enough speed and it can be done safely.
clean_q:
The photo is taken from the car you are driving.
You want to enter the motorway.
Which statement is correct?
A. You must drive to the very end of the acceleration lane.
B. You must give way.
C. You have priority when merging.
clean_e:
LESSON 4
When you enter a motorway via an acceleration lane,
you must give way to traffic already driving on the motorway.
That is why there is a yield (inverted triangle) road sign.
You are not required to drive to the end of the acceleration lane.
You may merge as soon as you have enough speed and it can be done safely.

This speed limit only applies on the exit.

q: Are you allowed to stop on the breakdown lane to text a message?
e:
LESSON 4 – ‘The motorway’.
You may not drive on a breakdown lane, not stand still and not park.
So do not stop to answer your mobile phone or to check the road map.
clean_q: Are you allowed to stop on the breakdown lane to text a message?
clean_e:
LESSON 4 – ‘The motorway’. 
You may not drive on a breakdown lane, not stand still and not park.

q:
When should you wear the fluorescent jacket in the event of a breakdown on a motorway?

A. Daytime only.
B. Only at night.
C. Always.
e:
LESSON 4 – ‘The motorway’.
The fluorescent jacket must be mandatory in the car and the driver must always wear it in the event of a breakdown.

clean_e:

The driveway is part of the motorway.

q:
There is heavy traffic on the motorway entrance ramp.
When are you allowed to merge?

A. You may merge as soon as it is safe to do so.
B. You must follow the cars in front of you to the end of the ramp before merging.
e:
LESSON 4
The zipper principle does not apply when entering a motorway.
So you do not have to drive to the end of the ramp before merging.
You may merge wherever it is safe to do so.
clean_q:
There is heavy traffic on the motorway entrance ramp.
When are you allowed to merge?
A. You may merge as soon as it is safe to do so.
B. You must follow the cars in front of you to the end of the ramp before merging.
clean_e:
LESSON 4
The zipper principle does not apply when entering a motorway.
So you do not have to drive to the end of the ramp before merging.
You may merge wherever it is safe to do so.

q:
A motorway with three lanes in your direction of travel.
Between which lanes can a motorcycle ride when there is a traffic jam?

A. Between lane 1 and 2.
B. Between lane 2 and 3.
C. He can choose.

q:
What is the maximum speed allowed in Wallonia on an ordinary road when using studded tyres?

A. 60 km/h.
B. 70 km/h.
C. 90 km/h.
e:
LESSON 4
Studded tyres may be used from 1 November to 31 March on vehicles with a maximum authorised mass of up to 3.5 tonnes.
The maximum permitted speed is:
- on motorways and roads with 2×2 lanes separated by a central strip: 90 km/h;
- on ordinary roads in all regions: 60 km/h.
clean_q:
What is the maximum speed allowed in Wallonia on an ordinary road when using studded tyres?

B. 70 km/h.
C. 90 km/h.
clean_e:

The maximum permitted speed is:

- on ordinary roads in all regions: 60 km/h.

q:
How should you react when driving on a motorway near roadworks?

A. Drive at a maximum speed of 30 km/h.
B. Always keep a lateral distance of 2 metres.
C. Drive carefully so as not to endanger the road workers.
e:
LESSON 4
When driving near roadworks on a motorway, you must:
- respect the applicable speed limits;
- keep a sufficient distance;
- drive carefully and attentively (watch out for workers, equipment and stationary vehicles);
- make sure you do not endanger or hinder anyone.
clean_q:
How should you react when driving on a motorway near roadworks?
A. Drive at a maximum speed of 30 km/h.
B. Always keep a lateral distance of 2 metres.
C. Drive carefully so as not to endanger the road workers.

q: When you place a warning triangle on the hard shoulder, other drivers travelling in your direction must see the triangle from a distance of (…) meters.
e:
LESSON 4 – ‘The motorway’.
When you stop on a breakdown lane, you have to put a warning triangle at least 100 meters behind your car immediately.
It must always be visible for oncoming traffic from a distance of 50 meters.

q:
Where must the reflective safety vest be located?

A. Inside the interior of the car.
B. In the trunk of the car.
e:
LESSON 4 - 'Motorway'
The reflective safety vest, also known as the yellow vest, must be located inside the vehicle’s interior, within easy reach, and not in the trunk. It is recommended to store it in the glove compartment, under the driver’s seat, or in a door pocket so that it is immediately accessible to the driver if they need to exit the vehicle on a motorway or a road for motor vehicles.
clean_q:
Where must the reflective safety vest be located?
A. Inside the interior of the car.
B. In the trunk of the car.
clean_e:
LESSON 4 - 'Motorway'
The reflective safety vest, also known as the yellow vest, must be located inside the vehicle’s interior, within easy reach, and not in the trunk. It is recommended to store it in the glove compartment, under the driver’s seat, or in a door pocket so that it is immediately accessible to the driver if they need to exit the vehicle on a motorway or a road for motor vehicles.

q: Is a reflective safety jacket obligatory in the car?
e:
LESSON 4 – ‘The motorway’.
. A reflective safety jacket
. A warning triangle.
. An extinguisher.
. A first aid kit.
clean_q: Is a reflective safety jacket obligatory in the car?
clean_e:
LESSON 4 – ‘The motorway’. 
. A reflective safety jacket
. A warning triangle.
. An extinguisher.
. A first aid kit.

q:
You didn’t go to a driving school, but your dad teaches you how to drive a car.
Are you allowed to practice/train on the motorway?
e:
LESSON 4 – ‘The motorway’.
Drivers with a provisional driving licence B must also exercise on the motorway.

q: Do you have to turn on the indicator on a motorway entrance?
clean_q: Do you have to turn on the indicator on a motorway entrance?

q:
You want to drive on to the motorway via the merging lane.

A. You accelerate as soon as possible on the merging lane.
B. You accelerate when you drive on the right lane.
e:
LESSON 4 – ‘The motorway’.
You have to accelerate as soon as possible on the merging lane.

q:
The black car (yellow circle) is allowed to drive maximum (…) km/hour on the motorway on that spot.

A. 70 km/hour.
B. 120 km/hour.
e:
LESSON 4 – ‘The motorway’.
This speed limit 70 km/hour only applies on the exit.

q:
What is the maximum permitted speed for a car with studded tyres on a motorway?

A. 70 km/h.
B. 90 km/h.
C. 120 km/h.
e:
LESSON 4
Studded tyres may be used between 1 November and 31 March on vehicles with a maximum authorised mass up to 3.5 tonnes.
Speed:
– Motorways and roads with 2×2 lanes separated by a median: maximum 90 km/h.
– Ordinary roads: maximum 60 km/h.

B. 90 km/hour.

LESSON 4 – ‘The motorway’.
The speed limit only applies to the bus lane.

q:
What‘s there at 1600 meters?

A. An interchange.
B. A junction.
C. End of the motorway.
e:
LESSON 4 – ‘The motorway’.
There are no junctions on a motorway. But there are places where two motorways merge. This place is called an interchange. Via an interchange you can drive from one motorway to another.
clean_q:
What‘s there at 1600 meters?
A. An interchange. 
B. A junction. 
C. End of the motorway.
clean_e:
LESSON 4 – ‘The motorway’. 
There are no junctions on a motorway. But there are places where two motorways merge. This place is called an interchange. Via an interchange you can drive from one motorway to another.

q:
There is a traffic jam. The grey car drives on the left lane on this motorway. A motorcycle wants to overtake between the left and the middle lane. Where should the car driver drive on the lane?

A. As far to the left as possible.
B. In the middle.
C. As far to the right as possible.
e:
LESSON 4 – ‘The motorway’.
In a queue motorcycles are allowed to ride:
. between the two left lanes of traffic.
. at a maximum speed of 20 km/hour faster than the other vehicles.
. and at a maximum speed of 50 km/hour.
So the grey car drives as far as possible to the left, to give place.
